WorkflowExecutionStartedResponse.description:
Emitted when a workflow execution begins.

This is the first event in any workflow execution lifecycle.

event_id.description: Unique identifier for this event instance.
event_id.title: Event Id

event_timestamp.description: Unix timestamp in nanoseconds when the event was created.
event_timestamp.title: Event Timestamp

parent_workflow_exec_id.description: Execution ID of the parent workflow that initiated this execution. If this is a root workflow, this field is not set.
parent_workflow_exec_id.title: Parent Workflow Exec Id

root_workflow_exec_id.description: Execution ID of the root workflow that initiated this execution chain.
root_workflow_exec_id.title: Root Workflow Exec Id

workflow_exec_id.description: Execution ID of the workflow that emitted this event.
workflow_exec_id.title: Workflow Exec Id

workflow_name.description: The registered name of the workflow that emitted this event.
workflow_name.title: Workflow Name

workflow_run_id.description: Run ID of the workflow execution. Changes on continue-as-new while workflow_exec_id stays the same.
workflow_run_id.title: Workflow Run Id

WorkflowExecutionStartedResponse.title: WorkflowExecutionStarted

Used for complete state snapshots or final results.

type.description: Discriminator indicating this is a raw JSON payload.

JSONPatchPayloadResponse.description:
A payload containing a list of JSON Patch operations.

Used for streaming incremental updates to workflow state.

type.description: Discriminator indicating this is a JSON Patch payload.
type.title: Type

value.description: The list of JSON Patch operations to apply in order.
value.title: Value

JSONPatchPayloadResponse.title: JSONPatchPayload

op.description: 'append' is an extension for efficient string concatenation in streaming scenarios.
op.title: Op

path.description: A JSON Pointer (RFC 6901) identifying the target location within the document. Can be a string path (e.g., '/foo/bar'), '/', '', or an empty list [] for root-level operations.
path.title: Path

value.description: The value to use for the operation. A string to append to the existing value
value.title: Value

path.description: A JSON Pointer (RFC 6901) identifying the target location within the document. Can be a string path (e.g., '/foo/bar'), '/', '', or an empty list [] for root-level operations.
